chapter_title.ar: باب الشَّكِّ فِي الْوُضُوءِ وَ مَنْ نَسِيَهُ أَوْ قَدَّمَ أَوْ أَخَّرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – The doubt in the Ablution (Wudhu) and the one who forgets it or brings something forward or delays

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/113)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/113)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتَ بَعْضَ وُضُوئِكَ فَعَرَضَتْ لَكَ حَاجَةٌ حَتَّى يَنْشَفَ وَضُوؤُكَ فَأَعِدْ وُضُوءَكَ فَإِنَّ الْوُضُوءَ لَا يَتَبَعَّضُ .

en.hubeali: ‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘When you perform part of your Ablution (Wudhu), so a need arises for you until your Ablution (Wudhu) dries up, so repeat your Ablution (Wudhu), for the Ablution (Wudhu) cannot be in parts’.
en.sarwar: 7. Through the chain of narrators as that of the previous Hadith is the following narration: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If while taking Wudu’ something happens that keeps you busy and your Wudu’ dries up, you must do your Wudu 5 again; it is not permissible to divide WuduY”
